source.title: The elastic constants and thermal expansion of single-crystal CdTe
source.journal: Journal of Physics D: Applied Physics

sample_and_measurement.specimen_form: plates with large faces on (110) planes
sample_and_measurement.thermal_expansion_method: strain gauge comparing sample expansion with Spectrosil B quartz
sample_and_measurement.temperature_sensor: gold +0.03 percent iron-chromel thermocouple calibrated against platinum resistance thermometer

published_thermal_expansion_evidence.figure_4_quantity: linear thermal expansion coefficient alpha along [100]
published_thermal_expansion_evidence.figure_5_quantity: fractional change in lattice parameter along [100] relative to 90 K
published_thermal_expansion_evidence.scope_interpretation: The paper states broad experimental coverage but publishes thermal-expansion graphical detail only around the 50-100 K anomaly. The printed article does not expose a numerical or graphical 100-293 K curve suitable for integration.

remaining_work: Acquire and hash Bagot, Granger, and Rolland 1993 or another primary CdTe source that publishes numerical or graphical thermal expansion over approximately 90-293 K.
Propagate the accepted full-range primary data to the Williams 293.15 K absolute lattice anchor.
Return to the Greenough-Palmer 79 K anomaly only if the final execution uncertainty becomes sensitive to the low-temperature integral.
